macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: India weighs steps to contain current account deficit as rupee and trade pressures build
items.summary: India is actively considering measures to prevent the current account deficit from widening further amid rupee weakness, higher import costs, and global economic uncertainty linked to the Iran war. Recent actions have included higher import duties on gold and silver and restrictions on some silver imports.

items.title: India's private sector growth eases in May as West Asia conflict weighs on sentiment
items.summary: A flash HSBC PMI reading showed private-sector activity in India softened to 58.1 in May, with the West Asia conflict cited as a drag. Services remained resilient even as broader growth momentum eased.

items.title: RBI greenlights record surplus transfer to government
items.summary: India's central bank approved a record surplus transfer of about Rs 2.86 lakh crore to the government, providing a sizable fiscal cushion. The transfer was described as one of the largest dividend payouts by the RBI.

items.title: Government relaxes insolvency rules for MSMEs
items.summary: New insolvency rules for MSMEs are set to simplify resolution by reducing costs and limiting conflicts of interest, including appointing only one valuer per asset class. The changes are effective from 2026.

items.title: India flags UK steel curbs at WTO as trade tensions remain elevated
items.summary: India and six other countries have raised concerns over UK steel restrictions at the WTO, highlighting ongoing trade friction. The issue adds to a broader backdrop of global protectionism and trade uncertainty.

items.title: U.S.-India trade deal seen finalizing in coming weeks
items.summary: The U.S. ambassador to India said the long-awaited trade deal is expected to be finalized in the coming weeks. The prospect of a deal could be a positive for trade sentiment and cross-border investment flows.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: 007 First Light launches this week with official release details and footage
items.summary: IO Interactive’s upcoming James Bond game, 007 First Light, is set to launch on May 27, with some regions getting access a day earlier. New official footage of the opening mission also surfaced after an early leak of the game’s first 13 minutes.

items.title: Destiny 2’s final major update and Bungie layoff fears dominate industry talk
items.summary: Reports indicate Destiny 2 will receive its final major update on June 9, after which active development may wind down. Jason Schreier’s reporting also fueled expectations of significant Bungie layoffs, with Destiny 3 reportedly not in active production.

items.title: Unreal Engine 6 revealed as Rocket League marks a “new era”
items.summary: A wave of gaming coverage highlights the reveal of Unreal Engine 6, alongside a new update-era announcement for Rocket League. The news has sparked discussion about what the engine shift could mean for future games and live-service titles.

items.title: Warhorse Studios says it is working on two new projects
items.summary: Warhorse Studios, best known for Kingdom Come: Deliverance, announced it is developing two new games. The reveal has drawn interest as one of the more substantial studio announcements from the latest gaming news cycle.

items.title: Mina the Hollower is nearing launch after multiple delays
items.summary: Yacht Club Games’ Mina the Hollower is scheduled to release this week following several delays. The studio’s financial situation has become part of the discussion, with reports saying it has largely exhausted funds generated by Shovel Knight.

items.title: Take-Two reportedly has multiple remakes and remasters planned
items.summary: Recent gaming industry chatter says Take-Two has six remakes and remasters planned over the next few years. The rumor adds to anticipation around the publisher’s broader release strategy.

items.title: Star Citizen crosses $1 billion in crowdfunding
items.summary: Star Citizen has reportedly passed the $1 billion crowdfunding milestone, making it one of the most heavily funded games ever. The news comes as a high-priced spaceship sale again drew attention to the project’s long development cycle.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Samsung unveils Galaxy Z TriFold with dual-hinge design and 10-inch unfolding display
items.summary: Samsung's newest foldable introduces a redesigned dual-hinge system, upgraded materials, and a larger 10-inch screen that unfolds from a pocketable form factor. The device is positioned as the company's most advanced foldable yet.

items.title: Google and Qualcomm push Android PCs toward the mainstream
items.summary: Google and Qualcomm are working to extend Android from phones to desktop and laptop computing, aiming to challenge Microsoft and Apple in personal computers. The effort could reshape the PC market if OEM support and software compatibility improve.

items.title: Amazon brings Alexa+ to the web
items.summary: Amazon is expanding Alexa+ beyond dedicated devices and onto the web, signaling a broader push to make its assistant available across more platforms. The move intensifies competition with other major digital assistant and chatbot products.

items.title: Qualcomm launches Snapdragon W5+ Gen 2 and W5 Gen 2 for wearables
items.summary: Qualcomm's latest wearable chip platforms focus on improving battery life, design flexibility, and connectivity for smartwatches and other devices. The update is aimed at removing key barriers that have limited wider wearable adoption.

items.title: Quantum Motion deploys first full-stack quantum computer built with standard silicon chip fabrication
items.summary: Quantum Motion says it has deployed a quantum computer using conventional silicon chip manufacturing, a milestone that could make scaling quantum systems more practical. The achievement is being watched as a potential step toward broader commercial viability.

items.title: Percona refocuses on structured database services for enterprises
items.summary: Percona is shifting emphasis toward faster, more structured database services as companies grapple with talent shortages and performance demands. The strategy is intended to help enterprises modernize open-source data platforms and prepare them for future workloads.

items.title: AMD's strong quarter highlights pressure on Intel and Nvidia
items.summary: AMD reported a $9.2 billion quarter, with leadership discipline and product execution helping the company gain ground in both enterprise AI strategy and competition with Intel. The results also underscore how AMD is navigating power constraints in the broader chip market.
